post.title: Fully Funded University Scholarships in Canada for Refugee and Asylum Seeker Students
post.excerpt: Are you a refugee or asylum seeker student looking to pursue higher education in Canada through fully funded university scholarships? You're in the right p
post.metaDescription: Are you a refugee or asylum seeker student looking to pursue higher education in Canada through fully funded university scholarships? You're in the right p
post.content:
Are you a refugee or asylum seeker student looking to pursue higher education in Canada through fully funded university scholarships? You're in the right place. Canada offers numerous scholarship opportunities for refugee and asylum seeker students, providing them with a chance to pursue their academic dreams without financial burdens. In this article, we will guide you through the available fully funded university scholarships in Canada, eligibility criteria, application processes, and more.

## Introduction to Fully Funded University Scholarships in Canada
Canada is known for its welcoming environment for international students, including refugees and asylum seekers. The country provides various scholarship programs to support these students in achieving their academic goals. Fully funded university scholarships in Canada cover tuition fees, living expenses, and other study-related costs, making it easier for refugee and asylum seeker students to focus on their studies.

## Who Qualifies
To be eligible for fully funded university scholarships in Canada as a refugee or asylum seeker, you must fall under one of the following visa categories:
* Refugee claimant
* Convention refugee
* Protected person
* Humanitarian-protected person
* Asylum seeker with a study permit
* Refugee with a study permit
These categories are subject to change, and it's essential to check the official government website for the most up-to-date information.

## Available Scholarships
Here are five fully funded university scholarships in Canada available for refugee and asylum seeker students:
### University of Toronto Scholarship for Refugees
The University of Toronto Scholarship for Refugees provides $50,000 per year for four years, covering tuition fees and living expenses. To be eligible, you must be a refugee or asylum seeker student with a strong academic record and a letter of admission from the University of Toronto. The deadline for application is January 15th of each year. For more information, visit [https://www.utoronto.ca](https://www.utoronto.ca).
### York University Scholarship for Refugee Students
The York University Scholarship for Refugee Students offers $40,000 per year for four years, covering tuition fees and living expenses. To be eligible, you must be a refugee or asylum seeker student with a strong academic record and a letter of admission from York University. The deadline for application is February 15th of each year. For more information, visit [https://www.yorku.ca](https://www.yorku.ca).
### University of British Columbia Scholarship for Refugees
The University of British Columbia Scholarship for Refugees provides $30,000 per year for four years, covering tuition fees and living expenses. To be eligible, you must be a refugee or asylum seeker student with a strong academic record and a letter of admission from the University of British Columbia. The deadline for application is March 15th of each year. For more information, visit [https://www.ubc.ca](https://www.ubc.ca).
### McGill University Scholarship for Refugee Students
The McGill University Scholarship for Refugee Students offers $45,000 per year for four years, covering tuition fees and living expenses. To be eligible, you must be a refugee or asylum seeker student with a strong academic record and a letter of admission from McGill University. The deadline for application is April 15th of each year. For more information, visit [https://www.mcgill.ca](https://www.mcgill.ca).
### University of Ottawa Scholarship for Refugee Students
The University of Ottawa Scholarship for Refugee Students provides $35,000 per year for four years, covering tuition fees and living expenses. To be eligible, you must be a refugee or asylum seeker student with a strong academic record and a letter of admission from the University of Ottawa. The deadline for application is May 15th of each year. For more information, visit [https://www.uottawa.ca](https://www.uottawa.ca).

## Application Process
To apply for fully funded university scholarships in Canada, follow these steps:
1. Research available scholarships and their eligibility criteria.
2. Check the website of your chosen university for scholarship information.
3. Prepare your application documents, including transcripts, language proficiency test results, and letters of recommendation.
4. Submit your application through the university's online portal or by mail.
5. Wait for the scholarship committee to review your application.
6. Receive an email notification of the scholarship decision.
7. Accept the scholarship offer and begin the study permit application process.
8. Register for classes and start your academic journey in Canada.

## Documents Checklist
When applying for fully funded university scholarships in Canada, make sure to have the following documents:
* Passport
* Study permit
* Language proficiency test results (e.g., IELTS or TOEFL)
* Transcripts
* Letters of recommendation
* Letter of admission from the university
* Proof of financial support (if required)

## Frequently Asked Questions
Here are some common questions asked by refugee and asylum seeker students:
What is the difference between a refugee and an asylum seeker?
Can I apply for a scholarship if I have a study permit?
How do I apply for a study permit in Canada?
What are the language proficiency requirements for Canadian universities?
Can I work part-time while studying in Canada?
How long does it take to process a scholarship application?
Can I apply for multiple scholarships at the same time?
What are the requirements for maintaining a scholarship in Canada?

## Additional Resources
For more information on fully funded university scholarships in Canada, visit the official government website at [https://www.canada.ca](https://www.canada.ca) or the University of Toronto's website at [https://www.utoronto.ca](https://www.utoronto.ca).

In conclusion, fully funded university scholarships in Canada provide refugee and asylum seeker students with an opportunity to pursue their academic dreams without financial burdens. To learn more about scholarship opportunities and application processes, visit our blog at https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog

related.funding: Full tuition plus living costs for doctoral studies in Canada
related.content:
## Introduction to Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships
The Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships are available for refugee students pursuing doctoral studies in Canada. These scholarships are designed to support students who have been displaced due to conflict, persecution, or natural disasters and are now seeking to continue their education in Canada. The scholarships provide full tuition plus living costs, enabling refugee students to focus on their studies and achieve their academic goals.

## Who Qualifies
To be eligible for the Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships, refugee students must hold a valid Canadian visa in one of the following categories:
* Protected Person (PP)
* Convention Refugee (CR)
* Country of Asylum (COA)
* Refugee Claimant (RC)
* Permanent Resident (PR) with prior refugee status
* Temporary Resident Permit (TRP) holder with prior refugee status
* other visa categories as specified by the scholarship provider

## Available Scholarships
The following are five scholarships available to refugee students in Canada:
### Trudeau Scholarship
The Trudeau Scholarship provides $40,000 per year for up to three years, covering tuition fees and living costs. Eligible students must be pursuing a doctoral degree at a Canadian university and demonstrate a commitment to social justice and human rights. The deadline for applications is November 15, 2026. For more information, visit https://www.trudeaufoundation.ca/en/programs/scholarships.
### Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarship
The Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarship provides $50,000 per year for up to three years, covering tuition fees and living costs. Eligible students must be pursuing a doctoral degree at a Canadian university and demonstrate academic excellence and research potential. The deadline for applications is November 1, 2026. For more information, visit https://vanier.gc.ca/en/home-accueil.html.
### Joseph-Armand Bombardier Scholarship
The Joseph-Armand Bombardier Scholarship provides $35,000 per year for up to two years, covering tuition fees and living costs. Eligible students must be pursuing a doctoral degree at a Canadian university and demonstrate a commitment to social sciences and humanities research. The deadline for applications is October 15, 2026. For more information, visit https://www.sshrc-crsh.gc.ca/funding-financement/programs-programmes/fellowships/bombardier/index-eng.aspx.
### Ontario Trillium Scholarship
The Ontario Trillium Scholarship provides $40,000 per year for up to four years, covering tuition fees and living costs. Eligible students must be pursuing a doctoral degree at an Ontario university and demonstrate academic excellence and research potential. The deadline for applications is October 1, 2026. For more information, visit https://www.ontario.ca/page/ontario-trillium-scholarship.
### University of Toronto Scholarship
The University of Toronto Scholarship provides $30,000 per year for up to four years, covering tuition fees and living costs. Eligible students must be pursuing a doctoral degree at the University of Toronto and demonstrate academic excellence and research potential. The deadline for applications is September 15, 2026. For more information, visit https://www.sgs.utoronto.ca/funding/graduate-funding-competition/.

## Application Steps
To apply for the Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships, follow these steps:
1. Review the eligibility criteria and ensure you meet the requirements.
2. Gather all required documents, including transcripts, letters of recommendation, and language proficiency test results.
3. Submit your application through the scholarship provider's online portal.
4. Complete the online application form and upload all required documents.
5. Ensure you meet the language proficiency requirements, either English or French.
6. Provide a personal statement outlining your research interests and goals.
7. Submit your application before the deadline.
8. Follow up with the scholarship provider to confirm receipt of your application.

## Documents Checklist
To apply for the Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships, you will need to provide the following documents:
* Valid Canadian visa
* Passport
* Transcripts
* Letters of recommendation
* Language proficiency test results
* Personal statement
* Research proposal
* Proof of admission to a Canadian university

## Frequently Asked Questions
1. What is the deadline for applying to the Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships?
The deadline for applying to the Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships is November 15, 2026. It is essential to submit your application before this date to be considered for the scholarship.
2. Can I apply for the Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships if I am not a Canadian citizen?
Yes, you can apply for the Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships if you are not a Canadian citizen. The scholarships are open to refugee students who hold a valid Canadian visa.
3. What is the value of the Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships?
The Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships provide full tuition plus living costs for doctoral studies in Canada. The exact value of the scholarship varies depending on the scholarship provider and the student's individual circumstances.
4. How do I apply for the Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships?
To apply for the Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships, you will need to submit an online application through the scholarship provider's website. You will need to provide all required documents, including transcripts, letters of recommendation, and language proficiency test results.
5. Can I apply for multiple scholarships at the same time?
Yes, you can apply for multiple scholarships at the same time. However, you will need to ensure that you meet the eligibility criteria for each scholarship and submit a separate application for each one.
6. What are the language proficiency requirements for the Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships?
The language proficiency requirements for the Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships vary depending on the scholarship provider. However, most scholarships require applicants to demonstrate proficiency in either English or French.
7. How long does it take to process my application?
The processing time for applications varies depending on the scholarship provider. However, it can take several weeks or even months to process an application.
8. Can I contact the scholarship provider for more information?
Yes, you can contact the scholarship provider for more information. The Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation has a dedicated team that can provide guidance and support throughout the application process.

## External Resources
For more information on the Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships, visit the official website of the [University of Toronto](https://www.utoronto.ca/) or the [Government of Canada](https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship.html).

## Conclusion
The Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships provide a unique opportunity for refugee students to pursue doctoral studies in Canada. With full tuition plus living costs covered, these scholarships enable students to focus on their studies and achieve their academic goals. For more information on scholarships and funding opportunities, visit https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog.

related.content:
## Introduction to Canada's Fully Funded Awards for Asylum Seekers
Canada's fully funded awards for asylum seekers pursuing undergraduate studies offer a life-changing opportunity for individuals who have been forced to flee their home countries. These awards provide financial support, covering full tuition fees and living costs, to help asylum seekers pursue their academic dreams in Canada. As an asylum seeker in Canada, you may be eligible for these awards, which can help you achieve your undergraduate degree without financial burden.

## Who Qualifies
To be eligible for Canada's fully funded awards for asylum seekers, you must be a holder of one of the following visa categories:
* Convention Refugee
* Protected Person
* Asylum Seeker with a positive Pre-Removal Risk Assessment (PRRA) decision
* Asylum Seeker with a positive Refugee Claim
* Asylum Seeker with a positive humanitarian and compassionate (H&C) decision
* Asylum Seeker with a positive temporary resident permit (TRP) decision
* Asylum Seeker who has applied for a study permit

## Available Scholarships
### University of Toronto National Scholarship Program
The University of Toronto National Scholarship Program offers a prestigious award that covers full tuition fees and living costs, valued at up to $80,000 per year, to undergraduate students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and creativity. To be eligible, you must be an asylum seeker who has been accepted into an undergraduate program at the University of Toronto. The deadline for application is November 2026, and you can find more information on the official website at https://www.utoronto.ca.

### University of British Columbia Presidential Scholars Award
The University of British Columbia Presidential Scholars Award is a highly competitive award that provides full tuition fees and living costs, valued at up to $70,000 per year, to undergraduate students who demonstrate academic excellence, leadership, and community involvement. To be eligible, you must be an asylum seeker who has been accepted into an undergraduate program at the University of British Columbia. The deadline for application is January 2027, and you can find more information on the official website at https://www.ubc.ca.

### McGill University Entrance Scholarship
The McGill University Entrance Scholarship offers a merit-based award that covers full tuition fees and living costs, valued at up to $60,000 per year, to undergraduate students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement. To be eligible, you must be an asylum seeker who has been accepted into an undergraduate program at McGill University. The deadline for application is January 2027, and you can find more information on the official website at https://www.mcgill.ca.

### University of Ottawa Scholarship for Asylum Seekers
The University of Ottawa Scholarship for Asylum Seekers provides a full scholarship that covers tuition fees and living costs, valued at up to $50,000 per year, to undergraduate students who are asylum seekers. To be eligible, you must be an asylum seeker who has been accepted into an undergraduate program at the University of Ottawa. The deadline for application is March 2027, and you can find more information on the official website at https://www.uottawa.ca.

### York University International Student Scholarship
The York University International Student Scholarship offers a partial award that covers tuition fees, valued at up to $20,000 per year, to undergraduate students who are international students, including asylum seekers. To be eligible, you must be an asylum seeker who has been accepted into an undergraduate program at York University. The deadline for application is May 2027, and you can find more information on the official website at https://www.yorku.ca.

## Application Steps
1. Check your eligibility for the scholarship by reviewing the visa categories listed above.
2. Research the universities and scholarships that are available to asylum seekers in Canada.
3. Review the application requirements and deadlines for each scholarship.
4. Prepare your application documents, including your academic transcripts, language proficiency test results, and letters of recommendation.
5. Submit your application before the deadline, either online or by mail, depending on the university's requirements.
6. Wait for the scholarship committee to review your application and make a decision.
7. If you are selected, you will be notified by the university and provided with instructions on how to accept the award.
8. Once you have accepted the award, you can begin making arrangements to enroll in your undergraduate program.

## Documents Checklist
* Academic transcripts
* Language proficiency test results (e.g. IELTS, TOEFL)
* Letters of recommendation
* Proof of asylum seeker status (e.g. positive refugee claim, PRRA decision)
* Proof of acceptance into an undergraduate program at a Canadian university
* Passport and study permit application documents

## Frequently Asked Questions
Q: What is the difference between a Convention Refugee and a Protected Person?
A: A Convention Refugee is someone who has been recognized as a refugee by the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR), while a Protected Person is someone who has been recognized as a protected person by the Government of Canada. Both categories are eligible for Canada's fully funded awards for asylum seekers.
Q: Can I apply for a scholarship if I have already started my undergraduate program?
A: Yes, you can apply for a scholarship even if you have already started your undergraduate program, but you must meet the eligibility requirements and apply before the deadline.
Q: How do I apply for a study permit as an asylum seeker?
A: You can apply for a study permit as an asylum seeker by submitting an application to Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) and providing the required documents, including proof of acceptance into an undergraduate program at a Canadian university.
Q: What is the language proficiency requirement for scholarships in Canada?
A: The language proficiency requirement for scholarships in Canada varies depending on the university and program, but most require a minimum IELTS score of 6.5 or a minimum TOEFL score of 90.
Q: Can I apply for multiple scholarships at the same time?
A: Yes, you can apply for multiple scholarships at the same time, but you must meet the eligibility requirements and apply before the deadline for each scholarship.
Q: How do I know if I am eligible for a scholarship as an asylum seeker?
A: You can review the eligibility requirements for each scholarship and check if you meet the requirements, including being an asylum seeker with a positive refugee claim or PRRA decision.
Q: What is the deadline for applying for scholarships in Canada?
A: The deadline for applying for scholarships in Canada varies depending on the university and program, but most deadlines fall between November and May of each year.

For more information on Canada's fully funded awards for asylum seekers, you can visit the official website of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) at https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship.html or the official website of the University of Toronto at https://www.utoronto.ca.

To learn more about the latest news and updates on scholarships for migrants, visit https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog.

related.content:
## Introduction to Canada's Fully Funded Masters Scholarships for Displaced Students
Canada's fully funded masters scholarships for displaced students provide a valuable opportunity for refugees and asylum seekers to pursue higher education in the country. These scholarships are specifically designed to support displaced students who have been affected by conflict, persecution, or natural disasters. The Canadian government and various universities offer these scholarships to help displaced students achieve their academic and career goals.

## Who Qualifies for Canada's Fully Funded Masters Scholarships
To qualify for Canada's fully funded masters scholarships, displaced students must meet specific eligibility criteria, including holding a valid visa category such as:
* Convention Refugee
* Protected Person
* Refugee Claimant
* Asylum Seeker
* Temporary Resident Permit holder
* Humanitarian-protected persons abroad

## Available Scholarships for Displaced Students
### University of Toronto Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program
The University of Toronto Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program provides full scholarships to displaced students pursuing a master's degree at the University of Toronto. The scholarship covers tuition fees, living expenses, and other costs up to $50,000 per year. Eligible students must have a bachelor's degree and demonstrate leadership skills and a commitment to giving back to their community. The deadline for application is February 1, 2027. For more information, visit [https://www.utoronto.ca](https://www.utoronto.ca).

### McGill University Scholarships for Displaced Students
McGill University Scholarships for Displaced Students offer full scholarships to displaced students pursuing a master's degree at McGill University. The scholarship covers tuition fees, living expenses, and other costs up to $40,000 per year. Eligible students must have a bachelor's degree and demonstrate academic excellence and leadership skills. The deadline for application is January 15, 2027. For more information, visit [https://www.mcgill.ca](https://www.mcgill.ca).

### University of British Columbia Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program
The University of British Columbia Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program provides full scholarships to displaced students pursuing a master's degree at the University of British Columbia. The scholarship covers tuition fees, living expenses, and other costs up to $45,000 per year. Eligible students must have a bachelor's degree and demonstrate leadership skills and a commitment to giving back to their community. The deadline for application is February 15, 2027. For more information, visit [https://www.ubc.ca](https://www.ubc.ca).

### Carleton University Scholarships for Displaced Students
Carleton University Scholarships for Displaced Students offer full scholarships to displaced students pursuing a master's degree at Carleton University. The scholarship covers tuition fees, living expenses, and other costs up to $35,000 per year. Eligible students must have a bachelor's degree and demonstrate academic excellence and leadership skills. The deadline for application is January 20, 2027. For more information, visit [https://www.carleton.ca](https://www.carleton.ca).

### York University Scholarships for Displaced Students
York University Scholarships for Displaced Students provide full scholarships to displaced students pursuing a master's degree at York University. The scholarship covers tuition fees, living expenses, and other costs up to $30,000 per year. Eligible students must have a bachelor's degree and demonstrate academic excellence and leadership skills. The deadline for application is January 25, 2027. For more information, visit [https://www.yorku.ca](https://www.yorku.ca).

## Application Steps for Canada's Fully Funded Masters Scholarships
To apply for Canada's fully funded masters scholarships, displaced students must follow these steps:
1. Research and choose a scholarship program that aligns with their academic and career goals.
2. Review and meet the eligibility criteria for the chosen scholarship program.
3. Prepare and gather all required documents, including academic transcripts and language proficiency test scores.
4. Submit an online application form and upload all required documents.
5. Pay the application fee, if applicable.
6. Provide proof of language proficiency, if required.
7. Provide proof of financial support, if required.
8. Submit the application and wait for the review process to be completed.

## Documents Checklist for Canada's Fully Funded Masters Scholarships
To apply for Canada's fully funded masters scholarships, displaced students must provide the following documents:
* Academic transcripts
* Language proficiency test scores (e.g., IELTS, TOEFL)
* Proof of language proficiency
* Proof of financial support
* Passport and valid visa
* Personal statement or letter of intent
* Letters of recommendation
* Resume or CV

## Frequently Asked Questions about Canada's Fully Funded Masters Scholarships
Q: What are the eligibility criteria for Canada's fully funded masters scholarships?
A: The eligibility criteria for Canada's fully funded masters scholarships vary depending on the scholarship program, but generally include holding a valid visa category, having a bachelor's degree, and demonstrating academic excellence and leadership skills. Displaced students must also meet the specific requirements of the chosen scholarship program.
Q: How do I apply for Canada's fully funded masters scholarships?
A: To apply for Canada's fully funded masters scholarships, displaced students must submit an online application form and upload all required documents, including academic transcripts and language proficiency test scores. They must also pay the application fee, if applicable, and provide proof of language proficiency and financial support, if required.
Q: What documents do I need to provide to apply for Canada's fully funded masters scholarships?
A: Displaced students must provide academic transcripts, language proficiency test scores, proof of language proficiency, proof of financial support, passport and valid visa, personal statement or letter of intent, letters of recommendation, and a resume or CV.
Q: How long does it take to process the application for Canada's fully funded masters scholarships?
A: The processing time for Canada's fully funded masters scholarships varies depending on the scholarship program, but generally takes several months to complete. Displaced students must submit their application well in advance of the deadline to ensure timely processing.
Q: Can I apply for multiple scholarship programs at the same time?
A: Yes, displaced students can apply for multiple scholarship programs at the same time, but must meet the eligibility criteria for each program and submit a separate application for each program.
Q: How much funding is available for Canada's fully funded masters scholarships?
A: The funding available for Canada's fully funded masters scholarships varies depending on the scholarship program, but generally covers tuition fees, living expenses, and other costs up to $50,000 per year.
Q: What are the language requirements for Canada's fully funded masters scholarships?
A: The language requirements for Canada's fully funded masters scholarships vary depending on the scholarship program, but generally require proficiency in English or French, as demonstrated by language proficiency test scores (e.g., IELTS, TOEFL).
Q: Can I bring my family with me to Canada if I am awarded a scholarship?
A: Yes, displaced students who are awarded a scholarship may be eligible to bring their family with them to Canada, depending on the scholarship program and the student's visa category.

For more information on Canada's fully funded masters scholarships for displaced students, visit the official government website at [https://www.canada.ca](https://www.canada.ca) or the University of Toronto website at [https://www.utoronto.ca](https://www.utoronto.ca). 
To learn more about scholarship opportunities for migrants and refugees, visit https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog.

related.title: Fully Funded PhD Scholarships for International Students in Canada
related.excerpt: Introduction to Fully Funded PhD Scholarships in Canada If you're an international student looking to pursue a PhD in Canada, you're in luck because there are n
related.metaDescription: Introduction to Fully Funded PhD Scholarships in Canada If you're an international student looking to pursue a PhD in Canada, you're in luck because there
related.content:
## Introduction to Fully Funded PhD Scholarships in Canada
If you're an international student looking to pursue a PhD in Canada, you're in luck because there are numerous fully funded PhD scholarships available for international students in Canada. These scholarships can cover your tuition fees, living expenses, and other costs associated with studying in Canada. The Canadian government and various universities offer these scholarships to attract top talent from around the world and provide opportunities for international students to pursue their academic goals.

## Who Qualifies for Fully Funded PhD Scholarships in Canada
To be eligible for fully funded PhD scholarships in Canada, you must fall under one of the following visa categories:
* Study permit holder
* Permanent resident
* Protected person
* Convention refugee
* Refugee claimant
* Temporary resident permit holder
* Foreign national with a temporary resident visa

## Available Fully Funded PhD Scholarships in Canada
### Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships
The Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships provide $50,000 per year for three years to international students pursuing a PhD in Canada. To be eligible, you must have achieved a first-class average in your previous two years of study and be nominated by a Canadian university. The deadline for application is November 1st, and you can find more information on the [official website](https://vanier.gc.ca/en/home-accueil.html).
### Trudeau Scholarships
The Trudeau Scholarships provide up to $40,000 per year for three years to international students pursuing a PhD in Canada. To be eligible, you must be a citizen of a country other than Canada and be pursuing a PhD in the social sciences or humanities. The deadline for application is December 15th, and you can find more information on the [official website](https://www.trudeaufoundation.ca/en/programs/trudeau-scholarships).
### Banting Postdoctoral Fellowships
The Banting Postdoctoral Fellowships provide $70,000 per year for two years to international students pursuing a postdoctoral fellowship in Canada. To be eligible, you must have completed a PhD within the last five years and be pursuing research in a Canadian university. The deadline for application is October 1st, and you can find more information on the [official website](https://banting.fellowships-bourses.gc.ca/en/home-accueil.html).
### Ontario Trillium Scholarships
The Ontario Trillium Scholarships provide $40,000 per year for four years to international students pursuing a PhD in Ontario, Canada. To be eligible, you must be an international student pursuing a PhD in a Ontario university and be nominated by the university. The deadline for application is March 1st, and you can find more information on the [official website](https://www.ouac.on.ca/ots/).
### Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarship
The Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarship provides up to $40,000 per year for three years to international students pursuing a PhD in Canada. To be eligible, you must be a citizen of a country other than Canada and be pursuing a PhD in the social sciences or humanities. The deadline for application is December 15th, and you can find more information on the [official website](https://www.trudeaufoundation.ca/en/programs/trudeau-scholarships).

## Application Steps for Fully Funded PhD Scholarships in Canada
1. Research and choose a Canadian university that offers your desired PhD program.
2. Check the eligibility criteria for the scholarship you're interested in.
3. Prepare your application package, including your resume, transcripts, and letters of recommendation.
4. Submit your application to the university and the scholarship provider.
5. Wait for the application to be processed and a decision to be made.
6. If you're selected, you'll receive an offer letter and instructions on how to accept the scholarship.
7. Apply for a study permit and other necessary documents to study in Canada.
8. Arrive in Canada and begin your PhD program.

## Documents Checklist
* Passport
* Transcript
* Resume
* Letters of recommendation
* Proof of language proficiency
* Proof of financial support
* Study permit application
* Scholarship application

## Frequently Asked Questions
1. What is the eligibility criteria for fully funded PhD scholarships in Canada?
The eligibility criteria varies depending on the scholarship provider, but generally includes a high academic standing, language proficiency, and a strong research proposal.
2. How do I apply for a study permit in Canada?
You can apply for a study permit online or through a paper application, and you'll need to provide documentation such as your acceptance letter, proof of financial support, and passport.
3. Can I work while studying in Canada?
Yes, international students can work part-time while studying in Canada, but you'll need to obtain a work permit and follow the regulations set by the Canadian government.
4. How long does it take to process a study permit application?
The processing time for a study permit application varies depending on the country you're applying from, but it can take several weeks or months.
5. Can I bring my family with me to Canada?
Yes, international students can bring their family with them to Canada, but you'll need to demonstrate that you can financially support them and they'll need to obtain the necessary visas.
6. What is the cost of living in Canada?
The cost of living in Canada varies depending on the city and province you're living in, but it's generally higher than in many other countries.
7. Can I stay in Canada after I graduate?
Yes, international students can stay in Canada after they graduate and work, or apply for permanent residency.
8. How do I find accommodation in Canada?
You can find accommodation in Canada by searching online, contacting your university, or working with a real estate agent.

For more information on studying in Canada, you can visit the [Government of Canada's website](https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship.html) or the [ Universities Canada website](https://www.univcan.ca/).

To learn more about PhD studies and available scholarships, visit the [MigrantScholar blog](https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog) for the latest updates and news.
